overview: This step replaces characters that are invalid in XML by a marker. It takes a raw document as input and sends back a raw document. The step recognizes raw characters as well as numeric character references (NCRs), such as &#000B; or . The output is done in the same encoding as the input document, and Byte-Order-Marks are preserved if present.

replacement.description: The string used to replace invalid XML characters. Must be a valid Java Formatter string (java.util.Formatter). The parameter passed is always the integer value of the Unicode code-point. For example, to replace the invalid character by its hexadecimal representation placed between braces use: '{%X}'. If you want to remove the invalid characters, leave the replacement string empty. Note that some characters like '%' have special function in the Java Formatter syntax and may need to be escaped (e.g., '%%' to produce a literal '%').
notes: The replacement string must conform to the Java Formatter specification (java.util.Formatter).
The parameter passed to the formatter is always the integer value of the Unicode code-point.
To remove invalid characters entirely, leave the replacement string empty.
Some characters like '%' have special function in the Java Formatter syntax and may need to be escaped.

processingNotes: You need to specify the input encoding of the document. The only case where encoding specification is not required is for files with a Byte-Order-Mark.
The output is done in the same encoding as the input document.
Byte-Order-Marks are preserved if they are present in the input.
The step recognizes both raw characters and numeric character references (NCRs), such as &#000B; or .

examples.title: Hexadecimal representation with prefix
examples.description: Replace invalid character U+000B with a 3-digit zero-padded hexadecimal representation prefixed with 'x'.

examples.title: NCR-like marker (default)
examples.description: Replace invalid character U+000B with an NCR-like marker using underscore prefix instead of ampersand.

examples.title: Simple character replacement
examples.description: Replace invalid character U+000B with a question mark.

examples.title: Unicode replacement character NCR
examples.description: Replace invalid character U+000B with the Unicode replacement character U+FFFD as an NCR.

examples.title: Decimal value with escaped percent signs
examples.description: Replace invalid character U+000B with its decimal code-point value surrounded by literal percent signs. The '%' characters must be escaped as '%%' in the Java Formatter syntax.

examples.title: Remove invalid characters
examples.description: To remove invalid characters entirely, leave the replacement string empty.
